contents:
# Former FBI Director James Comey surrenders on threat charge against Trump

## Overview
Former FBI Director James Comey surrendered to authorities on Wednesday facing a charge that an Instagram post he shared posed a threat to US President Donald Trump. The post showed seashells arranged as "86 47", slang for "get rid of" the 47th president. Comey denies wrongdoing and calls it political motivation.[1]

## Details
Prosecutors allege the post encouraged violence against Trump. This represents the second Justice Department criminal charge against Comey, a Trump critic; a prior false statements case remained dismissed. Comey appeared in federal court in Virginia following a grand jury indictment over the year-old post. He claims ignorance of the numbers' meaning.[1]

**Location**: Virginia, United States

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @BBCBreaking, which covers BBC breaking world news, conflicts, attacks, disasters. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following BBC breaking world news will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
